concept.title: What is DITA and how this starter project works
concept.shortdesc: This concept introduces DITA, explains map-driven chunking and embedded navigation, and shows how this starter repo layers XSLT customizations on top of the default HTML5 transform.

conbody.p: Welcome to . This sample set is published with DITA-OT 4.x to HTML5 using chunking and an embedded navigation TOC.

section.title: Filtering example (audience/platform)
section.p: DITA supports conditional processing using common attributes such as audience and platform. In this starter repo you can build multiple variants using DITAVAL files.
Novice tip: start by changing CSS first. You can get a working sidebar TOC with no XSLT at all using nav-toc=full.
Admin note: in production pipelines, pin your DITA-OT version per release line and run strict builds in CI to catch broken conrefs and links early.
Web-only guidance: add portal analytics via data-* attributes and load a small JS bundle for search, nav highlighting, or copy buttons.
Print-only guidance: consider reducing nav chrome, widening content, and using a dedicated print stylesheet or a different transform when generating paged deliverables.

section.title: What you will see in this HTML output

ul.li: Concept, task, and reference topic types.
Code blocks with Copy buttons (XSLT + JS).
A sticky sidebar TOC embedded in every page (nav-toc=full).
Consistent CSS hooks and data-dita-class attributes for instrumentation.

section.p: For more information about DITA-OT, see DITA-OT documentation.

section.title: Chunking model in the map
section.p: The map sets chunk="to-content" on the root branch under the “Getting started” heading. This is a common pattern: children become separate HTML pages while maintaining map navigation.
In practice, you’ll adjust chunking to match your information architecture (IA): chunk major procedures and references; keep tiny topics grouped if you prefer.

section.title: Inline elements and cross references
section.p: DITA supports semantic inline markup such as Settings, /etc/dita, and --nav-toc=full.
Inline phrases like product names and brand styling are typically rendered as <span> in HTML with stable CSS hooks.
Use cross references for internal links and set scope="external" or use absolute URLs for external links.

section.title: Figure and image
section.p: In a production theme, you often add a wrapper class around figures and images for consistent spacing, captions, and responsive scaling.

section.title: Code block example
section.p: This block will render with a Copy button in the starter plug-in.
